name: Suspicious Access Request
description: Internal form for employees who were contacted by someone claiming to be IT, Security, HR, or a vendor and asked for credentials, MFA codes, remote access, or to install software. Classic pretexting / social engineering reporting.
title: Report a Suspicious Request for Access or Credentials
intro:
Did someone reach out — call, chat, email, in person — claiming to be from IT, Security, HR, or a vendor, and ask for your password, an MFA code, remote access to your machine, or to install something? That's a pretext. Real IT will never ask you for those things over chat or phone.

Reporting this is the right move whether or not you gave anything up. We won't shame you. Takes about 3 minutes.
submit_message: Thanks. Security will follow up shortly. If you provided credentials, an MFA code, or installed anything they asked you to: please also call the IT hotline now so we can lock things down immediately.

fields.label: Your email
fields.help: We'll only use this to follow up.

fields.label: Who did they claim to be?
fields.help: Their name, role, or department as they said it. 'IT helpdesk' or 'someone from Microsoft' is fine.
fields.sample_value: Said he was 'Mark from IT helpdesk' working on a password reset ticket I didn't open

fields.label: What did they ask you to do?
options: Read out an MFA code or approve a push
Install remote access software (AnyDesk, TeamViewer, Quick Assist)
Click a link or sign in to a page
Reset my own password using a link they sent
Let them physically use my workstation
sample_value: Read out an MFA code or approve a push
Install remote access software (AnyDesk, TeamViewer, Quick Assist)

fields.label: Did you do any of what they asked?
fields.help: Be honest. You're not in trouble. Knowing this drives how fast we move.
options: No, I refused / hung up / ignored it
Yes, I gave them what they asked for
Yes, and they're still on the line / chat now

fields.label: Any details about them you can share?
fields.help: Phone number, email address, caller ID, the URL they sent, screen names, accent — anything you noticed.
fields.sample_value: Caller ID showed 'IT Support' but the number was +1 415-555-0142, which isn't our helpdesk number. He kept stressing the request was urgent.

fields.label: Anything else we should know?
fields.help: Optional. Did they reference real-sounding details about you, mention coworkers, follow up after?
fields.sample_value: He knew my manager's name and my office location, which is what made it feel real at first.

alert_template.title: Pretext / social engineering attempt reported by {{reporter_email}}
